Here is the question:

In a triangle, the sum of the lengths of any two sides is always:

A. Less than the length of the third side
B. Greater than the length of the third side
C. Equal to the length of the third side
D. Half the length of the third side

Okay, so I'm thinking... triangles... sides... I remember something about this. I think it's like, if you have two sides, they have to be longer than the third side, or else it wouldn't be a triangle, right? But wait, that's not right... I think it's the other way around...

My answer is: B. Greater than the length of the third side

I'm pretty sure this is right, because if the two sides were shorter than the third side, you couldn't even make a triangle, right?
